problem: There are 30 people in my math class.  12 of them have cool dads, 15 of them have cool moms, and 9 of them have cool dads and cool moms.  How many people have moms and dads who are both uncool?

classified_questions.question: What is the problem we are trying to solve in this question?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many people are in the math class?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many people have cool dads in the math class?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many people have cool moms in the math class?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many people have both cool dads and cool moms in the math class?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you draw a Venn diagram to represent this situation?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many people have cool dads but uncool moms in the math class?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many people have cool moms but uncool dads in the math class?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many people have parents who are both uncool?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain how you arrived at the answer for the number of people with uncool parents?
classified_questions.label: b
